article_title: Bipartisan Bill Seeks Warrant Requirements for Section 702 Searches as FISA Surveillance Authority Nears April Expiration

content_preview.title: Bipartisan Bill Seeks Warrant Requirements for Section 702 Searches as FISA Surveillance Authority Nears April Expiration
content_preview.summary: Senators Lee and Wyden introduce the Government Surveillance Reform Act to close warrantless search loopholes and ban government data broker purchases, with Section 702 set to expire on April 20, 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A bipartisan group of lawmakers introduced the Government Surveillance Reform Act on March 12, seeking to overhaul Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act before the authority expires on April 20, 2026. The bill would require federal agents to obtain warrants before searching Americans' communications collected under the program and would prohibit the government from purchasing personal data from commercial brokers without judicial approval.

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive News article covering Section 702 reform legislation with excellent detail on provisions, political context, and civil liberties positions.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources verified. EFF confirms 3.4M warrantless searches stat, bill analysis, and EFF's cautious position. Brookings confirms April 20 expiration, warrant amendment failure, Carter Page context, and compliance improvements. ACLU confirms opposition to clean reauthorization. Note: the '90 organizations' coalition claim attributed to ACLU could not be confirmed from the specific page fetched, though ACLU's stance is verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims confirmed across sources. Legislative details, political dynamics, and civil liberties positions all accurately represented.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Presents both reform advocates' and intelligence officials' perspectives.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Section 702 or FISA coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong digital rights/policy News article with verified sources and balanced coverage. Approved.
